body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Update to use new ClimaTimeSteppers interface implemented in https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaTimeSteppers.jl/tree/ts/cts-refactor

## Content
- [x] remove SciMLBase dependency, use ClimaTimeSteppers throughout
- [x] add a function `convert_cb` so we can accommodate `ClimaDiagnostics.DiagnosticsCallback`s, which are still `SciMLBase.DiscreteCallback`s (this function will be removed after we make a release of ClimaDiagnostics switching to use `CTS.DiscreteCallback`)
- [x] bump the minimum allowed Thermodynamics version see [this PR](https://github.com/CliMA/Thermodynamics.jl/pull/322) for details why

## Compat changes
It looks like with SciMLBase (and its compat) removed there are more possible combinations of packages versions, so the downgrade tests are having trouble. To fix this I added the `forcedeps` option to the downgrade test, which more closely replicates the v1 behavior (see [this discussion](https://github.com/julia-actions/julia-downgrade-compat/issues/22)). I also had to update the compat entries for some packages (and made the Thermodynamics backport release linked above)

<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: rm SciMLBase; use new CTS interface
